name: self-test
description: Pattern for testing your own code during implementation. Ensures quality before declaring complete.
capabilities: Run specific test files or affected tests
Execute lint checks with automatic fixes
Run type checking for TypeScript or Python
Pre-complete checklist before marking work done
author: Clouder0
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing testing pattern guidance. All 14 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting markdown code block delimiters as shell backticks and JSON description fields as cryptographic patterns. The skill contains no executable code, scripts, or network operations.
valueStatement: Catch issues early with an iterative testing workflow. Run tests, lint, and typecheck after each code change before declaring work complete.
limitations: Does not create test files automatically
Relies on project-specific run-tests and lint skills
Requires existing test infrastructure in the project
Commands vary by programming language and framework

useCases.title: Verify each code change
useCases.description: Run tests after writing new code to catch bugs immediately.

useCases.title: Catch issues early
useCases.description: Use the test-lint-typecheck loop before moving to the next task.

useCases.title: Maintain code quality
useCases.description: Ensure code meets quality standards before integration.

promptTemplates.title: Run tests after changes
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the tests for the file I just modified and report the results.

promptTemplates.title: Full verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Run tests, lint, and typecheck on my recent changes and fix any issues found.

promptTemplates.title: Specific test file
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the test file for {module_name} to verify the implementation.

promptTemplates.title: Continuous checking
promptTemplates.prompt: After each code change, run the self-test loop to ensure quality before proceeding.

bestPractices: Run tests immediately after writing new code, not at the end
Fix failures right away instead of accumulating them
Use the complete checklist before marking work as pre-complete
antiPatterns: Batching all testing until the end of implementation
Ignoring test failures and continuing anyway
Skipping tests because the code seems to work

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: Any language with test, lint, and typecheck tools including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, and Rust.

faq.question: What if my project has no tests?
faq.answer: This skill requires existing test infrastructure. Set up tests first using your framework's testing tools.

faq.question: Can I use this with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes, the same commands used locally (npm test, pytest) typically run in CI environments.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads files and runs test commands you specify. No data leaves your machine.

faq.question: Why are my tests failing after small changes?
faq.answer: This is the intended behavior. Small, incremental testing helps identify exactly which change introduced an issue.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual testing?
faq.answer: Automated tests run faster, catch regressions, and document expected behavior. Manual testing cannot match this reliability.
